channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Leadership Behavior is Causing an AI Adoption Gap
item.description: AI delivers when leaders don’t just delegate but also lead its adoption.

item.title: Spirit Airlines plans a much smaller future as it tries to survive bankruptcy — again
item.description: Spirit Airlines is hanging on by a thread –but it is hanging on. The budget airline announced a plan Tuesday that would put it on track to exit its se...

item.title: How to scale genomics to transform precision diagnostics
item.description: Unlocking the power of genetics to provide meaningful answers to patients when they matter most is at the crux of precision diagnostics. As technologi...

item.title: Why your best-looking candidates often perform the worst
item.description: Eight times the output. Same job. Same title. Not 80%. 800%. That’s a lot. And yet, most hiring systems and processes are almost perfectly designed to...

item.title: How one AI company is helping businesses navigate Trump’s new tariff chaos following the Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Plus a chaotic AI Impact Summit wraps up in India and Anthropic accuses Chinese rivals of using Claude's answers to train their own models

item.title: A New FDA Plan Could Transform Treatment Access for Rare Disease Patients
item.description: The framework was released as draft guidance on February 23.

item.title: What Makes You a ‘Lightweight’ Drinker? New Research Has an Answer
item.description: A new study tracked the impact wine has on brain connectivity and function.

item.title: Domino’s Turned a Money‑Saving Hack for Customers Into a $4.4 Billion Business
item.description: In its Q4 2025 earnings call, Domino’s revealed the engine quietly driving most of its traffic growth.

item.title: Warner Bros. is reviewing a new offer from Paramount as the takeover fight heats up
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery says it’s reviewing a new takeover offer from Paramount , but it continues to recommend a competing proposal from Netflix to it...

item.title: Trump is set to throw another tariff tantrum at the state of the union. Economists know better
item.description: Trump calls himself "Tariff Man" and is moving to pull another rabbit out of his hat. Don't be confused.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says these ‘dumb things’ in our current economy could point to another financial crisis
item.description: Jamie Dimon, the CEO of JPMorgan Chase, is sounding the alarm bell, warning investors that he is starting to see some similarities between today’s fin...

item.title: ‘The state of our union is more indebted than ever’: Budget watchdog continues war of words with Trump White House
item.description: The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget took heat over the weekend, with Scott Bessent saying its leader “should be ashamed.”

item.title: Why We’re Installing Systems Earlier Than We’d Like
item.description: Systems are not about scale, they’re about stress.

item.title: Gen Z’s enthusiasm for all things touchable is resurrecting the analog economy—and costing parents
item.description: Live Nation had its biggest year yet for concert attendance. Print book sales are at a record high. The vinyl record industry has enjoyed a resurrecti...

item.title: The workplace benefit 95% of workers want but aren’t satisfied with is a pretty basic one: bereavement leave, study shows
item.description: Research from Empathy’s 2026 Workplace Benefits Report, shared exclusively with Fortune, underscores a “clear gap” between what workers need what empl...
